overview.short: The Sree Balakrishna Swamy Temple, Kuzhuppilly of Goud Saraswat Brahmin (G.S.B.) community is in Kuzhuppilly village, Vypeen Island, Ernakulam district, Kerala, India.
overview.description: The Sree Balakrishna Swamy Temple, Kuzhuppilly of Goud Saraswat Brahmin (G.S.B.) community is in Kuzhuppilly village, Vypeen Island, Ernakulam district, Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Kuzhuppilly
topPlaces.description: Kuzhuppilly is a suburb of Kochi city and a tourist destination in Vypin Island in Kerala, India. The areas in Kuzhuppilly village are Pallathamkulangara, Cheruvaipu, Thundippuram, Manappally, and Ayyampilly.

topPlaces.name: Ezhikkara
topPlaces.description: Ezhikkara is a Village in the North Paravur Taluk of Ernakulam District, Kerala. The North Paravur to Chathanad road (9 km) passes through this village.

topPlaces.name: Cherai Gowreeshwara Temple
topPlaces.description: The Cherai Gowreeshwara Temple is a Hindu temple in the village of Cherai on Vypeen island in the state of Kerala in South India.

topPlaces.name: Vypin
topPlaces.description: Vypin (IPA: [ʋɐi̯pːin], Cochin Portuguese: Isla Santa) is one of the group of islands that form part of the city of Kochi, in the Indian state of Kerala. Vypin forms a barrier island which lies between the Arabian Sea in the west and the Kerala backwaters (Cochin backwaters) formed by the various distributaries of Periyar river, in the east.

topPlaces.name: Chakkarakadavu
topPlaces.description: Chakkarakadavu is a small village to the east of the town of Cherai, on Vypin Island in Ernakulam district, Kerala, India. Chakkarakadavu shares a common history with Cherai and with the greater Vypin Island.

topPlaces.name: Don Bosco Church, North Paravur
topPlaces.description: Don Bosco Church at North Paravur, Kerala, is a church of Paravur Parish under the Roman Catholic (Latin) Diocese of Kottappuram. Situated near Perumpadanna Junction at Paravur-Cherai road, this is one of the few churches in India named after Don Bosco.

topPlaces.name: Cherai Beach
topPlaces.description: Cherai Beach is located in Cherai on the northern side of Vypin Island, a suburb of the city of Kochi in the state of Kerala, India. One of the most visited beaches in the state, it is situated around 25 km (15 mi) from downtown Kochi and 20 km (12 mi) from Cochin International Airport.

topPlaces.name: Kuriapilly
topPlaces.description: Kuriappilly now known as Kuriappilly, Kuriappilly South, labor Junction, Shappupadi (Toddy parlor ferry, Kappelapadi. Kuriapilly was a border of Travancore kingdom in the British India, the name Kuriapilly is old up to 500 years, even in the letters of Tippu Sultan we can see the name Kuriapilly.

The Cherai Gowreeshwara Temple is a Hindu temple in the village of Cherai on Vypeen island in the state of Kerala in South India.

Vypin (IPA: [ʋɐi̯pːin], Cochin Portuguese: Isla Santa) is one of the group of islands that form part of the city of Kochi, in the Indian state of Kerala. Vypin forms a barrier island which lies between the Arabian Sea in the west and the Kerala backwaters (Cochin backwaters) formed by the various distributaries of Periyar river, in the east.

Chakkarakadavu is a small village to the east of the town of Cherai, on Vypin Island in Ernakulam district, Kerala, India. Chakkarakadavu shares a common history with Cherai and with the greater Vypin Island.

Don Bosco Church at North Paravur, Kerala, is a church of Paravur Parish under the Roman Catholic (Latin) Diocese of Kottappuram. Situated near Perumpadanna Junction at Paravur-Cherai road, this is one of the few churches in India named after Don Bosco.

Cherai Beach is located in Cherai on the northern side of Vypin Island, a suburb of the city of Kochi in the state of Kerala, India. One of the most visited beaches in the state, it is situated around 25 km (15 mi) from downtown Kochi and 20 km (12 mi) from Cochin International Airport.

Kuriappilly now known as Kuriappilly, Kuriappilly South, labor Junction, Shappupadi (Toddy parlor ferry, Kappelapadi. Kuriapilly was a border of Travancore kingdom in the British India, the name Kuriapilly is old up to 500 years, even in the letters of Tippu Sultan we can see the name Kuriapilly.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sree Balakrishna Swami Temple, Kuzhuppilly?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sree Balakrishna Swami Temple, Kuzhuppilly is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sree Balakrishna Swami Temple, Kuzhuppilly?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (22 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (18 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sree Balakrishna Swami Temple, Kuzhuppilly?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sree Balakrishna Swami Temple, Kuzhuppilly at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sree Balakrishna Swami Temple, Kuzhuppilly?
faq.a: Stays in Sree Balakrishna Swami Temple, Kuzhuppilly start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sree Balakrishna Swami Temple, Kuzhuppilly?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kuzhuppilly, Ezhikkara, Cherai Gowreeshwara Temple, Vypin, Chakkarakadavu.
